metadata.title: Operation Spalax Transportation Systems Attack Scenario

Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Complex Operation Spalax aims to disrupt transportation systems by targeting key personnel, exploiting vulnerabilities in web applications, and gaining access to sensitive areas.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets File Process Hacking Tool Command Scheduled Task Command & Control Server Application Log Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) Windows Service Public IP/Network Block Network Traffic Network Share DNS Record
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method File Metadata (Mechanism) Reg.exe Utility Custom Tool Development Command Execution (Mechanism) Scheduled Task/Job Web Shell Application Log Content (Mechanism) SIP and Trust Provider Hijacking Keylogging Network Service Scanning Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism) File Access (Mechanism) Network Share Access (Mechanism) Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker uses resource fork to gather information about transportation systems.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker identifies vulnerabilities in transportation system networks using network sniffing.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attacker creates custom tools for exploitation.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker targets key personnel with spearphishing links to obtain login credentials.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker exploits vulnerabilities in web applications used by transportation employees.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-663: Exploitation of Transient Instruction Execution
stage.description: The attacker runs malicious code on compromised systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker schedules tasks to maintain persistence.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC-650: Upload a Web Shell to a Web Server
stage.description: The attacker maintains access through web shells.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker gains elevated privileges to access sensitive areas.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.technique_name: T1198: SIP and Trust Provider Hijacking
stage.description: The attacker disables anti-virus software to avoid detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker steals login credentials of key personnel.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker maps out network services and identifies potential targets.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attacker moves laterally within the network using remote services.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker collects sensitive data from compromised systems.

stage.name: Collection
stage.technique_name: T1039: Data from Network Shared Drive
stage.capec: CAPEC-643: Identify Shared Files/Directories on System
stage.description: The attacker collects sensitive data from network traffic.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates stolen data over alternative protocols.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts transportation systems to cause maximum impact.

Scenario.summary: Operation Spalax is a complex attack scenario targeting transportation systems, involving multiple stages of reconnaissance, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.
